semantic.role: Defines code pattern recognition rules for the ArchSpine mirror system
responsibilities: Specifying patterns for import statements (single and grouped)
Specifying patterns for exportable code elements: functions, structs, interfaces, type aliases, variables, constants
Specifying patterns for usage relationships: function calls and selector expressions
Mapping recognized patterns to semantic kinds (e.g., Function, Class, Interface, Variable, Type)
outOfScope: Non-Go programming languages or syntax extensions
Build system or dependency resolution logic
Code generation, refactoring, or runtime analysis
Specific versioning or compatibility constraints beyond pattern syntax

English.purpose: This document provides the pattern configuration that enables ArchSpine's mirror layer to identify and extract structural code elements from Go source files. It defines a declarative mapping between text patterns (e.g., import grouping, function signatures, struct definitions) and the semantic categories used by the mirror for tracking design, dependencies, and architecture.
English.context_and_audience: This configuration is intended for developers and maintainers of the ArchSpine system who need to extend, customize, or verify the code recognition capabilities for Go projects. It assumes familiarity with Go syntax and the ArchSpine mirror architecture. The patterns are written in a simple DSL using placeholders ($SOURCE, $NAME, etc.) and optional grouping constructs.
key_takeaways: The file defines three top-level sections: imports, exports, and usages, each with a list of pattern objects.
Each pattern object has a unique id and a pattern string; exports additionally include a kind (Function, Class, Interface, Variable) to categorize discovered elements.
The patterns use a specific placeholder syntax (e.g., $NAME, $$$ARGS, $$$BODY) to match code fragments with variable content.
This configuration is language-specific (Go) and can be extended with additional patterns as needed.
